theme_strategy: Bulkpe-style Admin Dashboard Theme (Light/Cream base, Purple Primary, Soft Pastels)

typography.headings: Manrope or Work Sans. Do not use generic Inter or Space Grotesk. Font weights should be distinct (e.g., font-semibold to font-bold).
typography.body: Manrope or IBM Plex Sans. Keep line-height relaxed for data readability.
typography.rules: Use tight letter spacing (tracking-tight) for large headers. Use uppercase + wide spacing (tracking-widest) for tiny overlines/labels.

colors.primary: #7C3AED (Tailwind purple-600) - Use for primary actions, active states, and focus rings.
colors.background_main: #FDFCF9 or Tailwind stone-50 (Light cream/off-white) - Use for the main admin content area.
colors.background_sidebar: #FFFFFF - Crisp white for the sidebar to separate it from the cream background.
colors.accent_peach: #FFEDD5 (Tailwind orange-100) - For soft metric cards or badges.
colors.accent_blue: #DBEAFE (Tailwind blue-100) - For soft metric cards or badges.
colors.accent_lavender: #F3E8FF (Tailwind purple-100) - For soft metric cards, active sidebar links.
colors.buttons: Black (#000000) or Purple (#7C3AED) with white text. Hover states should lighten the hue.
colors.forbidden_colors: Teal green or teal blue (#1B93A4) are completely prohibited.

visual enhancers.borders: 1px solid #E2E8F0 (Slate 200) for cards and sidebar borders. Avoid heavy drop shadows.
visual enhancers.border_radius: Rounded corners heavily used. Buttons: rounded-full or rounded-xl. Cards: rounded-2xl.
visual enhancers.surfaces: Use flat solid colors for cards. Do not use glassmorphism or pure transparent backgrounds for any overlays, dropdowns, or modals. Always use solid white (bg-white) for dropdowns with a subtle border.
visual enhancers.active_states: Sidebar active items should have a soft purple background (bg-purple-50) with purple text (text-purple-600) and potentially a left border highlight.

grids.dashboard_stats: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-6. Use the pastel accent colors for these stat cards.
grids.dashboard_content: grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6. Main content takes col-span-2, side feed/summary takes col-span-1.

layouts.admin_shell: Flex layout with a fixed 250px-300px white sidebar (bg-white, border-r border-slate-200) and a flexible main content area taking up remaining space with a cream background (bg-stone-50).
layouts.spacing: Generous spacing. Use p-6 to p-8 inside cards. Page wrappers should have p-8 or p-10.
layouts.alignment: Left-align dense content, forms, and tables. Keep optical alignment in mind.

motion.micro_interactions: Subtle -translate-y-[2px] on card hover, scale-95 on button click.
motion.transitions: Use specific properties: transition-colors duration-200 ease-in-out for buttons and links. DO NOT use 'transition-all'.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text over pastel backgrounds meets minimum 4.5:1 contrast. Use dark text (slate-800 or purple-900) on pastel cards.
accessibility.focus: All interactive elements must have focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-purple-600 focus-visible:ring-offset-2.
accessibility.testing: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute (e.g., data-testid='admin-sidebar-users-link').

media.icons: Use lucide-react (default) or @phosphor-icons/react for a clean, professional dashboard look. Icon sizes should be consistent (w-5 h-5 for sidebar).

3d libraries.usage: Not recommended for this administrative dashboard. Prioritize clarity and data density with Recharts or standard Shadcn UI charts.

instructions_to_main_agent: CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: Apply these styling rules ONLY to pages and components within the admin flow (e.g., files starting with Admin, like AdminLayout.js, AdminDashboard.js). Do NOT modify global CSS files (index.css/App.css) in a way that alters user-facing pages.
Use scoped Tailwind utility classes directly in the Admin React components.
For the Shadcn components used in the Admin flow, override their default styles using className props rather than rewriting the base component, to avoid affecting the user flow.
Ensure the layout has a solid white sidebar, cream/off-white main content area, and uses the defined pastel colors for specific data cards or badges.
All components are JavaScript (.js) not TypeScript (.tsx). Follow this strictly when editing or creating files.
Ensure NO transparent backgrounds are used on dropdowns, modals, or headers. They must have solid background colors.
Implement data-testid attributes on all new or modified interactive elements.
For icons, use `lucide-react`. Ensure proper spacing between icon and text.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
Components MUST use named exports; Pages MUST use default exports.
